intro.line: Now then, where were we? Ah yes... the battle of wits.
Nobody can tell yet. That is rather the point of a battle of wits.
Ah, the Dread Pirate Roberts. I was beginning to think you weren't coming.
Now, a battle of wits to the death. *cackles* Poison is on the table. Genius is not — at least on your side.
*sneeze* Is this the part where he figures out the cup?
Not yet. First he must prove himself worthy. There are riddles.

riddlePhase.line: I suppose you think yourself clever. How TIRESOME.
*cough* Grandpa, is Vizzini always this... loud?
He does love the sound of his own voice, yes.
I have never lost a battle of wits. NEVER. It is a point of professional pride.

gobletPhase.line: Choose. But choose wisely. Or poorly. I honestly have no preference.
*cough* I'd pick the one on the left.
You're taking a very long time. Is this a strategy? It is a very bad one.

outro_win.line: And so the Dread Pirate Roberts proved his worth. The battle of wits was won.
He beat Vizzini? *cough* But Vizzini's supposed to be the smartest man in the world!
Only in his own estimation, which is the loneliest kind of genius.
Vizzini never did learn the first rule of a battle of wits: never assume you're the smartest one at the table.
Buttercup was rescued. Vizzini was outwitted. And the goblet chose wisely.
In his experience, Vizzini was always angry. It was his natural resting state.

outro_lose.line: Well. That did not go as hoped.
...Would you like to hear a different part of the story?
Vizzini was, it must be said, very smug about the whole business.
Briefly. As villains sometimes do. But that is a story for another night.
The iocane powder is odorless, tasteless, and entirely fatal. He should have known.
Maybe next time he'll be more careful.
Next time he will be. Sleep now — we'll try again tomorrow.

riddle:correct: INCONCEIVABLE! A lucky guess. Don't mistake fortune for intellect.
You got it right. The stopped clock is right twice a day. This is your twice.
Correct. I find this development mildly irritating.
Well. Yes. That is technically right. I hope you don't expect a celebration.
Hmph. You stumbled into the correct answer like a blind man into a library. Accidentally useful.
riddle:wrong: WRONG! As I fully expected! Shall we continue your education in failure?
Ha! Not even close. I've met turnips with better reasoning.
Incorrect. Are you even trying? This is insulting — and I am Vizzini, I have high standards for insults.
Wrong! And might I say, spectacularly wrong. There is an artistry to this level of failure.
You answered that with such confidence. That confidence was entirely misplaced. Wrong.
hint:requested: Asking for help? INCONCEIVABLE! And yet here we are.
You need Buttercup's assistance? I am embarrassed for you. Truly.
Running to a woman for help. How very... you.
Of course you need a hint. Of course. Please, take all the time you need to be wrong anyway.
Asking for help is the last refuge of the inferior mind. Which explains everything.
goblet:correct: ...This is not possible.
But... I chose both goblets so carefully... INCONCEIVABLE!
You— how— I had accounted for everything! EVERYTHING!
Never go in against a Sicilian... wait. You're not— this isn't—
I don't understand. I studied. I prepared. I— *thud*
goblet:poisoned: HAHAHA! You fool! You fell victim to one of the classic blunders!
Did you think I would make it easy? Did you actually think that?
Iocane powder! Odourless, tasteless, and I spent the last year building up an immunity! You have not.
You see, what I did was — well, it doesn't matter now, does it?
The greatest of these blunders is this: never go in against a Sicilian when DEATH is on the line! HAHAHAHA!

hint:requested: Westley — listen carefully. The clues are in his words.
Think, my love. Vizzini always tells you more than he means to.
I've been watching him. His words — remember his words.
You can do this. What has he said that seemed strange? Start there.
Trust yourself. And think about what you've seen and heard today.
goblet:correct: Oh, Westley! I knew you could do it!
You did it! You clever, wonderful man!
I never doubted you. Not for a moment. Well — perhaps for a moment.
Oh thank goodness. I had a plan if you failed but I didn't like it very much.
Westley! You're brilliant! You've always been brilliant!
goblet:poisoned: Westley, no— Are you... can you hear me? Westley?
He'll be alright. He has to be. The resistance— Westley!
I told you. I told you to trust the clues. Oh, Westley.
This isn't over. It isn't over.
Westley! Stay with me — the iocane won't— this isn't— breathe!

goblet:correct: And so the Dread Pirate Roberts chose... wisely.
He had listened. He had paid attention. And it had been enough.
Victory. As unlikely as it sometimes seemed.
The battle of wits concluded in the only way a good story allows.
He had done it. Against Vizzini. Against the odds. He had done it.
goblet:poisoned: The wrong goblet. And yet — the story was not over.
Iocane, as it turned out, had been survived before.
A setback. A painful, poisonous setback. But not the end.
He had drunk deeply of the wrong cup. But resistance, it seems, can be built.
And yet. And yet, the Dread Pirate Roberts remained standing. Barely. But standing.

goblet:correct: *cough* He did it! He actually did it!
YES! *sneeze* Sorry. But yes!
*cough* That was so good. Can we do it again?
I knew he'd pick right. I knew it. *sneeze*
That's the best part of the whole story! *cough* Well, one of them.
goblet:poisoned: *cough* Wait. Did he— is he okay?
*sneeze* That's not how it's supposed to go.
Grandpa... *cough* ...does he make it?
*very quiet cough*
*cough* I don't like this part.
